featured.title: GTA 6 developer Rockstar Games confirms data breach, says there is no impact on operations - VideoCardz.com
featured.summary: GTA 6 developer Rockstar Games confirms data breach, says there is no impact on operations  VideoCardz.com
featured.reason: Major data breach with ransom threat
featured.suggestedTitle: Rockstar Games Hit by Data Breach: Ransom Threat Looms
featured.suggestedSummary: Rockstar Games, the developer behind the highly anticipated GTA 6, has confirmed a data breach by a malicious group threatening to leak confidential data unless a ransom is paid by April 14. While Rockstar claims the breach is 'non-material' to company data or gamer info, the attackers have reportedly accessed sensitive information. Security teams should monitor for potential leaks and assess the impact on their systems, especially if using Rockstar's services.

quickNews.title: Over 20,000 crypto fraud victims identified in international crackdown
quickNews.summary: An international law enforcement action led by the U.K.'s National Crime Agency (NCA) has identified over 20,000 victims of cryptocurrency fraud across Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States. [...]

quickNews.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
quickNews.summary:
Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation surveillance system called Webloc.
The tool was developed by Israeli company Cobwebs Technologies and is now sold by its successor Penlink after the two firms merged in July 2023
